source_title: Green versus brown: Comparing the employment impacts of energy efficiency, renewable energy, and fossil fuels using an input-output model
verified_value: ~7,490 jobs created in RE and related industries (4,500 direct + 2,990 indirect) from $1B reallocation; net economy-wide gain of ~4,840 jobs after fossil fuel sector contraction of 2,650 jobs (940 direct + 1,710 indirect)
verified_metric_definition: Net employment effect (I-O simulation) of shifting $1 billion from fossil fuel subsidies to public procurement/spending on renewable energy (e.g. government buildings). Captures job gains in RE supply chain minus losses in fossil fuel industries.
direct_vs_total: total (direct + indirect); net of cross-sector losses
notes: ACCEPT (illustrative policy example). Page 8. Complements the per-$1M multipliers by showing net macro effect of reallocation (not just gross). source_title: Infrastructure Australia
verified_value: ~$90 billion Infrastructure Investment Program (IIP) as core component of broader $120 billion land transport infrastructure investment pipeline (Commonwealth commitment 2023-24 to 2032-33)
verified_metric_definition: Scale of Commonwealth land transport infrastructure co-investment program (IIP) delivered via National Partnership Agreement (NPA) with states/territories. Part of 10-year pipeline; Commonwealth accountable for expenditure under IIP.
projected_vs_realised: committed / projected (pipeline; some projects realised, majority forward)
notes: ACCEPT_SCALE (headline Australian public infra investment). Page 15. Authoritative official figure for national land transport spend. Useful benchmark for size of government infrastructure programs vs private (e.g. data centres, energy). Note: IIP is subset of larger pipeline; states/territories provide matching funding. Jobs/Indigenous outcomes reported separately in report but not in this specific quote.
context_quote: The Commonwealth has indicated it will invest $120 billion from 2023–24 to 2032–33 in land transport infrastructure through the infrastructure investment pipeline. The around $90 billion Infrastructure Investment Program forms a part of this pipeline, and is implemented through a NPA with states and territories, where the Commonwealth selects projects to co-invest in with the States. The Commonwealth is accountable for project expenditure under the IIP.

source_title: Infrastructure Australia
verified_value: $120 billion 10-year infrastructure investment pipeline (2023-24 to 2032-33) for land transport under National Partnership Agreement / IIP framework
verified_metric_definition: Total Commonwealth land transport infrastructure investment pipeline over decade, encompassing IIP and related programs. Delivered via NPA; states/territories implement projects with co-funding.
projected_vs_realised: projected / committed (multi-year pipeline with annual drawdowns)
notes: ACCEPT_SCALE (key headline pipeline). Page 22 / 29. Central planning figure for Australian transport infra. Multiple extractions (0026, 0027) from different pages; consolidate. Provides macro context for public investment scale, risk allocation challenges, and delivery capacity. Jobs outcomes (e.g. Indigenous) modelled elsewhere in report.
context_quote: The NPA consolidates several arrangements for providing funding to the state and territory infrastructure sectors into a single agreement. It specifically covers land transport infrastructure projects, with the exception of research and evaluation funding projects, that are delivered through the IIP, which forms part of the broader $120 billion, 10-year infrastructure investment pipeline. Figure 5 sets out the funding sources that comprise the pipeline.
